concept_name: Equipotential Surfaces — Same-V Shells, ⟂ to the Field, No Work Along Them
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.2 Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance (§2.7 Equipotential surfaces) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (start from ONE same-V point P → ask where the OTHER same-V points are → they close into one surface → nest the whole family of surfaces, 3D spheres not flat rings, like a trekking-map contour → slide a charge ALONG one surface and confront the moving-always-costs-work belief: force is radial, motion tangential, W = F·d·cos90° = 0 → overlay the field lines and watch them pierce every shell at exactly 90°, inner high-V to outer low-V → relabel the shells to EQUAL V-steps and watch them crowd near the charge: dense means strong field → flip the source sign and see only the SIGN and field direction flip, geometry untouched → hand the explorer the draggable point P: V is constant on a shell, jumps across shells), never copied. The atomic idea: an equipotential surface is the locus of all points sharing one common potential V; for a point charge these are concentric spheres (r = kQ/V); the electric field is everywhere PERPENDICULAR to them and points from high V toward low V; NO work is done moving a charge ALONG one (W = F·d·cos90° = 0), while moving BETWEEN surfaces costs W = qΔV; and for equal V-steps the surfaces CROWD where the field is strong (r ~ 1/V). SCOPE: this is the GEOMETRY-of-constant-V diamond of Ch.2 — concentric spheres, E ⟂ surface, zero work along, spacing↔field-strength. It declares electric_potential_meaning (what V MEANS, V = W/q), electric_potential_point_charge (the VALUE/FORMULA V = kQ/r), and electric_field_point_charge (the VECTOR E = kQ/r²) as prerequisites and does NOT re-teach them. It STOPS SHORT of the value V = kQ/r itself, the equipotentials of a dipole or a uniform field, and conductors / capacitance. NEW field_3d equipotential primitives reuse the point_charge_positive scenario + the electric_potential_meaning shell/explorer primitives: V-labelled concentric shells with per-state reveal, a per-state shells_override for the equal-V-step crowding relabel, a tangential slide_along_shell rig with the cos90° corner glyph, a show_field_lines_cross_shells overlay with right-angle ticks at every crossing, a sign_flip recolor (draws_arrow:false), and a draggable equipotential_explorer with a live V readout that is constant on a shell and jumps across shells. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; the four misconceptions are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal).

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture a trekking map of the Himalayas or the Western Ghats, the kind with brown contour lines. Each contour joins points at the same HEIGHT. Walk ALONG a contour and you stay level — you do no work against gravity. Cross the contours and you climb or descend — that costs work. And where the contours BUNCH close together the slope is steep. Equipotential surfaces are exactly this map for electric potential: each surface joins points at the same V, walking along one is free, crossing them costs work, and bunched surfaces mean a strong field.
real_world_anchor.secondary: In a thunderstorm, the safest place is inside the metal body of a car or a bus. The whole metal shell sits at one single potential — it is one big equipotential surface — so there is no potential DIFFERENCE across the people inside, and the dangerous current runs around the outside skin, not through them. Same-V everywhere on the shell is what keeps the occupants safe.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: The big shiny metal dome of a Van de Graaff generator — like the one at the Birla Science Museum in Hyderabad — is charged to a huge voltage. Every point on that dome's surface is at the SAME potential: the dome is a single equipotential surface, which is why the charge spreads evenly over it and the field just outside is perpendicular to the metal.

Q.name: Source charge at the centre (kept at +1 across the geometry arc; its SIGN flips in the sign-flip state)

r.name: Distance of the field point P from the source charge (the STATE_7 drag owns this; drives which shell P sits on and the live V readout; r = 1.5 puts P on the V = 8 shell)

k.name: Coulomb constant k = 1/(4*pi*epsilon_0) (the k in V = k*Q/r and r = k*Q/V)

epsilon_0.name: Permittivity of free space (a fixed constant of nature)

DEMO_V_PER_NC.name: Demo potential readout per demo-nC of source charge at unit demo radius — THE round number the shell tables ride on (12 gives V = 12/8/6/4 at r = 1/1.5/2/3 and the equal-V-step V = 12/9/6/3 at r = 1/1.333/2/4)
DEMO_V_PER_NC.unit: demo V per (demo nC) at r=1

charge_sign.name: Sign of the source charge (+1 positive shells / -1 negative shells) — flips V's sign and the field direction without ever touching the geometry

formulas.equipotential_locus: an equipotential surface is the locus of all points at one common V
formulas.point_charge_shells: r = k*Q/V (concentric spheres, one per V value)
formulas.field_perpendicular: E is perpendicular to the equipotential, pointing from high V to low V
formulas.equal_step_spacing: for equal V-steps, r ~ 1/V, so surfaces crowd near the charge

constraints: The equipotential surfaces of a point charge are concentric spheres, one per V value, at radius r = k*Q/V (C1).
The potential V is a SCALAR: one number per point, no arrow, and every point on a single surface shares that same V (C2).
No work is done moving a charge ALONG an equipotential surface: the force is radial, the motion is tangential, so W = F*d*cos(90 deg) = 0 (C3).
Work IS done moving a charge BETWEEN surfaces: W = q*(V2 - V1) = q*delta_V, the per-charge work across the potential difference (C4).
The electric field E is everywhere PERPENDICULAR to the equipotential surface and points from HIGH V to LOW V, never tangential to the surface (C5).
Drawn for EQUAL V-steps, the surfaces obey r ~ 1/V: they CROWD near the charge (strong field) and SPREAD far out (weak field) - spacing reads off field strength (C6).
Two distinct equipotential surfaces (different V values) can never cross or touch, because a single point cannot hold two different potentials at once (C7).
Flipping the source charge's sign flips the SIGN of every V and reverses the field direction, but it does NOT change the geometry - the surfaces stay the same concentric spheres (C8).

aha_moment.statement: The field crosses every equipotential at exactly 90°, pointing toward lower V.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Eight green radial field lines pierce the cyan concentric shells, each crossing marked with a small right-angle tick, every line running from the inner high-V shell outward to the outer low-V shell.

STATE_1.title: One surface, one V — where are the other points reading 8?

scene_composition.text: one shell blooms through P: V = 8
natural guess: V = 8 belongs to just point P
all points at this distance: one equipotential surface

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is a point charge. One single shell blooms through point P, and its potential reads V equals 8.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक point charge है। एक single shell point P से होकर खिलता है, और इसका potential V equals 8 read करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ ఒక point charge ఉంది. ఒక single shell, point P గుండా వికసిస్తుంది, మరియు దాని potential V equals 8 అని చదువుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every point on this shell shares that same value, V equals 8. The natural guess is that this reading of 8 belongs to just the one point P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इस shell पर हर point उसी value को share करता है, V equals 8. natural guess यह है कि 8 का यह reading केवल एक point P का है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ shell పై ప్రతి point అదే value ను share చేస్తుంది, V equals 8. natural guess ఏమిటంటే 8 అనే ఈ reading కేవలం ఒక point P కు మాత్రమే చెందుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: They form a whole surface. Every point at this distance from the charge reads V equals 8 — that surface is one equipotential.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वे एक पूरी surface बनाते हैं। charge से इस distance पर हर point V equals 8 read करता है — वह surface एक equipotential है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అవి ఒక మొత్తం surface ను ఏర్పరుస్తాయి. charge నుండి ఈ distance వద్ద ప్రతి point V equals 8 అని చదువుతుంది — ఆ surface ఒక equipotential.

misconception_watch.belief: A reading of V = 8 belongs to just the one point P, not to a whole set of points spread around the charge.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The single shell through P blooms into a full closed surface, every point on it carrying the same V = 8 label - one VALUE, a whole locus of points at the same distance.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: An equipotential surface is the locus of ALL points sharing one common V, not a single point.

STATE_2.title: The whole family — nested spheres, not flat rings

scene_composition.text: closer in V is higher, farther out V is lower
natural guess: flat rings drawn around the charge
nested SPHERES: V = 12, 8, 6, 4 stepping outward
like a trekking map: each contour = equal height

tts_sentences.text_en: That one shell is not alone. Closer in the potential is higher, farther out it is lower.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वह एक shell अकेला नहीं है। पास में potential ज़्यादा होता है, दूर बाहर कम होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ ఒక shell ఒంటరిగా లేదు. దగ్గరగా potential ఎక్కువగా ఉంటుంది, దూరంగా బయట తక్కువగా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Picture the full family. The natural guess is that these are flat rings drawn around the charge, like circles on paper.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पूरे family की कल्पना कीजिए। natural guess यह है कि ये charge के चारों ओर खींची गई flat rings हैं, कागज़ पर circles की तरह।
tts_sentences.text_te: మొత్తం family ను ఊహించండి. natural guess ఏమిటంటే ఇవి charge చుట్టూ గీసిన flat rings, కాగితంపై circles లాంటివి.

tts_sentences.text_en: In three dimensions they are nested SPHERES, not rings. Here they are: V equals 12, 8, 6, 4 as you step outward.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीन dimensions में ये nested SPHERES हैं, rings नहीं। ये रहे: V equals 12, 8, 6, 4 जैसे-जैसे आप बाहर step करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు dimensions లో ఇవి nested SPHERES, rings కాదు. ఇవిగో: మీరు బయటికి step చేస్తున్నప్పుడు V equals 12, 8, 6, 4.

tts_sentences.text_en: It is exactly like a trekking map. Each contour line joins points of equal height; here each sphere joins points of equal potential.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह बिल्कुल एक trekking map की तरह है। हर contour line equal height के points को जोड़ती है; यहाँ हर sphere equal potential के points को जोड़ता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది సరిగ్గా ఒక trekking map లాంటిది. ప్రతి contour line equal height ఉన్న points ను కలుపుతుంది; ఇక్కడ ప్రతి sphere equal potential ఉన్న points ను కలుపుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Equipotential surfaces are flat planes or flat rings drawn around the charge, like circles on paper.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The 3D family fades in as nested concentric SPHERES (not rings), and the trekking-map contour anchor names them as closed level-sets wrapping the charge.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: For a point charge the equipotentials are nested 3D SPHERES, one per V value, never flat rings.

STATE_3.title: No work along the surface — W = F·d·cos90° = 0

scene_composition.text: slide a charge ALONG one shell, tangent to it
natural guess: sliding along costs work
force radial, motion tangential — they meet at 90°
W = F · d · cos 90° = 0 — sliding along is FREE

tts_sentences.text_en: Slide a charge along one shell, tangent to the surface, from here to there.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक charge को एक shell के साथ slide कीजिए, surface के tangent, यहाँ से वहाँ तक।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charge ను ఒక shell వెంట slide చేయండి, surface కు tangent గా, ఇక్కడ నుండి అక్కడికి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Moving a charge usually costs work. So the natural guess is that sliding it ALONG this one shell costs work too.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक charge को move करना आमतौर पर work लेता है। तो natural guess यह है कि इसे इस एक shell के साथ ALONG slide करने में भी work लगता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charge ను move చేయడం సాధారణంగా work తీసుకుంటుంది. కాబట్టి natural guess ఏమిటంటే దీన్ని ఈ ఒక shell వెంట ALONG slide చేయడంలో కూడా work అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the angle. The force is radial, straight out; the motion is tangential, along the surface. They meet at ninety degrees.
tts_sentences.text_hi: angle देखिए। force radial है, सीधा बाहर; motion tangential है, surface के साथ। वे ninety degrees पर मिलते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: angle ను చూడండి. force radial గా ఉంది, నేరుగా బయటికి; motion tangential గా ఉంది, surface వెంట. అవి ninety degrees వద్ద కలుస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the work is W equals F times d times cos ninety degrees, and cos ninety is zero. Sliding along an equipotential is free.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो work है W equals F times d times cos ninety degrees, और cos ninety zero है। एक equipotential के साथ slide करना free है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి work అనేది W equals F times d times cos ninety degrees, మరియు cos ninety zero. ఒక equipotential వెంట slide చేయడం free.

misconception_watch.belief: Moving a charge always costs work, so sliding it from one point to another along a surface must take energy.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The amber charge slides tangentially along the shell while the radial force and the tangential displacement are marked meeting at a 90 degree corner tick, and the callout reads W = F*d*cos90 = 0.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Along an equipotential the force is perpendicular to the motion, so W = F*d*cos90 = 0 - sliding along is free.

STATE_4.title: Field crosses at 90° — inner high-V to outer low-V

scene_composition.text: field lines run straight out from the charge
natural guess: the lines run ALONG the shells
they pierce every sphere at exactly 90°
inner high-V → outer low-V: field points downhill

tts_sentences.text_en: Now bring back the field lines, running straight out from the charge, and overlay them on the shells.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब field lines वापस लाइए, charge से सीधे बाहर जाती हुई, और उन्हें shells पर overlay कीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు field lines ను తిరిగి తీసుకురండి, charge నుండి నేరుగా బయటికి వెళ్తూ, మరియు వాటిని shells పై overlay చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The natural guess is that the field lines run ALONG the surfaces, lying flat against them.
tts_sentences.text_hi: natural guess यह है कि field lines surfaces के ALONG चलती हैं, उन पर flat पड़ी हुई।
tts_sentences.text_te: natural guess ఏమిటంటే field lines surfaces వెంట ALONG నడుస్తాయి, వాటిపై flat గా పడి ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: They pierce every sphere at exactly ninety degrees. The field is perpendicular to the equipotential, everywhere.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वे हर sphere को बिल्कुल ninety degrees पर pierce करती हैं। field equipotential के perpendicular है, हर जगह।
tts_sentences.text_te: అవి ప్రతి sphere ను సరిగ్గా ninety degrees వద్ద pierce చేస్తాయి. field equipotential కు perpendicular గా ఉంది, ప్రతిచోటా.

tts_sentences.text_en: And the lines run from the inner high-V shell to the outer low-V shell — the field always points from high potential to low.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और lines inner high-V shell से outer low-V shell तक चलती हैं — field हमेशा high potential से low की ओर इशारा करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు lines inner high-V shell నుండి outer low-V shell వరకు నడుస్తాయి — field ఎల్లప్పుడూ high potential నుండి low కు సూచిస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The field lines run ALONG the equipotential surfaces, lying flat against them.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Eight radial field lines pierce the cyan shells with a right-angle tick at every crossing, each line running from the inner high-V shell outward to the outer low-V shell - never tangential.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The field is PERPENDICULAR to the equipotential, crossing it at exactly 90 degrees, high V to low V.

STATE_5.title: Crowded means strong — equal V-steps bunch near the charge

scene_composition.text: relabel for EQUAL steps: V = 12, 9, 6, 3
natural guess: evenly spaced like ladder rungs
they bunch near the charge, spread far out
crowded = strong field; spread = weak field

tts_sentences.text_en: Relabel the shells so the steps are EQUAL: V equals 12, 9, 6, 3 — a drop of three each time.
tts_sentences.text_hi: shells को फिर से label कीजिए ताकि steps EQUAL हों: V equals 12, 9, 6, 3 — हर बार तीन की drop।
tts_sentences.text_te: shells ను మళ్ళీ label చేయండి, తద్వారా steps EQUAL గా ఉంటాయి: V equals 12, 9, 6, 3 — ప్రతిసారి మూడు తగ్గుదల.

tts_sentences.text_en: The natural guess is that these equal steps are evenly spaced, like rungs on a ladder.
tts_sentences.text_hi: natural guess यह है कि ये equal steps evenly spaced हैं, ladder की rungs की तरह।
tts_sentences.text_te: natural guess ఏమిటంటే ఈ equal steps evenly spaced గా ఉంటాయి, ladder మీద rungs లాగా.

tts_sentences.text_en: They bunch near the charge and spread far out, even though every step is the same three volts.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वे charge के पास bunch करते हैं और दूर बाहर spread करते हैं, भले ही हर step वही तीन volts हो।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి step అదే మూడు volts అయినప్పటికీ, అవి charge దగ్గర bunch అవుతాయి మరియు దూరంగా spread అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Crowded shells mean a strong field; widely spread shells mean a weak field. Spacing reads off the field strength.
tts_sentences.text_hi: crowded shells का मतलब strong field; widely spread shells का मतलब weak field। spacing field strength बताती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: crowded shells అంటే strong field; widely spread shells అంటే weak field. spacing field strength ను తెలియజేస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Equipotential surfaces are always evenly spaced, like rungs on a ladder, no matter the field.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Drawn at EQUAL V-steps of three volts, the shells visibly bunch close together near the charge (r ~ 1/V) and spread far apart at large r - tight spacing where the field is strong.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Equal V-steps give UNEQUAL spacing (r ~ 1/V): surfaces crowd where the field is strong, spread where it is weak.

STATE_6.title: Flip the sign — only sign and direction flip, geometry stays

scene_composition.text: flip the source from +Q to −Q
natural guess: the surfaces warp or rotate
V labels go negative, source and shells recolor blue
geometry never moves — still concentric spheres

tts_sentences.text_en: Flip the source charge from plus to minus. The shells are still here, same nested spheres.
tts_sentences.text_hi: source charge को plus से minus में flip कीजिए। shells अभी भी यहाँ हैं, वही nested spheres।
tts_sentences.text_te: source charge ను plus నుండి minus కు flip చేయండి. shells ఇప్పటికీ ఇక్కడ ఉన్నాయి, అవే nested spheres.

tts_sentences.text_en: The natural guess is that the surfaces warp or rotate when the source charge flips.
tts_sentences.text_hi: natural guess यह है कि source charge flip होने पर surfaces warp या rotate करती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: natural guess ఏమిటంటే source charge flip అయినప్పుడు surfaces warp లేదా rotate అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Only the sign changes, not the shape. Every V label turns negative, and the source and its shells recolor to the negative blue.
tts_sentences.text_hi: केवल sign बदलता है, shape नहीं। हर V label negative हो जाता है, और source और उसके shells negative blue में recolor हो जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: కేవలం sign మారుతుంది, shape కాదు. ప్రతి V label negative అవుతుంది, మరియు source మరియు దాని shells negative blue కి recolor అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The geometry never moves. The shells stay concentric spheres — still toward lower V, just lower V is now inward.
tts_sentences.text_hi: geometry कभी नहीं हिलती। shells concentric spheres ही रहते हैं — अभी भी lower V की ओर, बस lower V अब अंदर है।
tts_sentences.text_te: geometry ఎప్పుడూ కదలదు. shells concentric spheres గానే ఉంటాయి — ఇప్పటికీ lower V వైపు, కేవలం lower V ఇప్పుడు లోపల ఉంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Flipping the source charge makes the equipotential surfaces warp, rotate, or carry a direction of their own.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: On the flip the shells keep the exact same concentric-sphere geometry; only the V labels gain a minus sign and the field lines reverse to point inward - no warp, no rotation, no arrow on V.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A sign flip changes only the SIGN of V and the field direction; the geometry stays the same concentric spheres.

STATE_7.title: You drive — V is constant on a shell, jumps across shells

scene_composition.text: drag the point P in and out — watch the live V
move along one shell: V stays constant
cross to the next shell: the reading JUMPS

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive. Drag the point P in and out and watch the live V readout.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप drive करें। point P को अंदर और बाहर drag कीजिए और live V readout देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు drive చేయండి. point P ని లోపలికి మరియు బయటికి drag చేయండి మరియు live V readout ని చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Move it anywhere on one shell — the V reading stays constant, because the whole shell is one potential.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसे एक shell पर कहीं भी move कीजिए — V reading constant रहती है, क्योंकि पूरा shell एक potential है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాన్ని ఒక shell పై ఎక్కడైనా move చేయండి — V reading constant గా ఉంటుంది, ఎందుకంటే మొత్తం shell ఒక potential.

tts_sentences.text_en: Cross to the next shell and the reading JUMPS. You have stepped from one equipotential to a different one.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अगले shell पर cross कीजिए और reading JUMPS करती है। आप एक equipotential से दूसरे पर step कर गए हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: తదుపరి shell కు cross చేయండి మరియు reading JUMPS అవుతుంది. మీరు ఒక equipotential నుండి వేరొక దానికి step చేశారు.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered equipotential surfaces when they can (1) define an equipotential surface as the locus of all points at one common potential V; (2) recognise that for a point charge these surfaces are concentric spheres; (3) explain that no work is done moving a charge ALONG an equipotential because W = F*d*cos90 = 0, while moving BETWEEN surfaces costs W = q*delta_V; (4) state that the electric field is everywhere PERPENDICULAR to the equipotential and points from high V to low V; (5) read the spacing of equal-V-step surfaces as a map of field strength (crowded = strong, spread = weak, r ~ 1/V); and (6) reason that two distinct equipotential surfaces can never intersect.

options.A: The set of all points that share one common value of the potential V
options.B: The set of all points where the electric field E has the same strength
options.C: A surface on which the charge density is constant
options.D: The single path a test charge would follow if released from rest

distractor_misconceptions.B: confuses equal-potential with equal-field-strength - a surface of constant V is not a surface of constant E
distractor_misconceptions.C: confuses equal-potential with equal charge-density on the surface
distractor_misconceptions.D: imagines a trajectory a released charge follows, not a static locus of equal-V points

questions.tested_idea: An equipotential surface is the locus of all points at one common potential V.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Define an equipotential surface in terms of the quantity that is the same at every point on it.

questions.stem: What shape are the equipotential surfaces around a single point charge?

options.A: Concentric spheres centred on the charge
options.C: Straight radial lines pointing out from the charge
options.D: A single sphere of fixed radius around the charge

distractor_misconceptions.B: uses the uniform-field picture (parallel planes) instead of the point-charge picture
distractor_misconceptions.C: confuses the equipotential surfaces with the radial FIELD lines
distractor_misconceptions.D: shows only one surface, missing the whole nested family of spheres

questions.tested_idea: Around an isolated point charge the equipotentials are concentric spheres, r = kQ/V.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Around an isolated point charge, what is the geometric form of the surfaces of constant potential?

questions.stem: How much work is done moving a charge a distance d ALONG one equipotential surface, and why?

options.A: Zero, because the displacement is perpendicular to the force, so W = F*d*cos90 = 0
options.B: W = F*d, because the charge is pushed through a distance d
options.C: W = q*V, where V is the potential of that surface
options.D: It depends on the speed at which the charge is moved

distractor_misconceptions.B: ignores the angle between force and displacement, treating the motion as along the force
distractor_misconceptions.C: uses the absolute potential V instead of the potential DIFFERENCE (which is zero along one surface)
distractor_misconceptions.D: wrongly makes the work depend on speed rather than on force, displacement, and angle

questions.tested_idea: No work is done along an equipotential because W = F*d*cos90 = 0.
questions.parallel_form_stem: You carry a charge from one point to another, both on the same equipotential surface. State the work done and justify it with the angle between force and displacement.

questions.stem: At what angle does the electric field cross an equipotential surface, and which way does it point?

options.A: Exactly 90 degrees (perpendicular), pointing from high V toward low V
options.B: Exactly 90 degrees, pointing from low V toward high V
options.C: Zero degrees - the field runs along the surface
options.D: Some angle between 0 and 90 degrees that depends on the distance

distractor_misconceptions.B: gets the perpendicularity right but reverses the direction (field actually points high V to low V)
distractor_misconceptions.C: the confronted misconception - imagines the field running ALONG the surface instead of crossing it
distractor_misconceptions.D: thinks the crossing angle varies with distance instead of being a fixed right angle

questions.tested_idea: The field is perpendicular to the equipotential (90 degrees) and points from high V to low V.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Describe the orientation of the electric field relative to an equipotential surface, and state which direction along that perpendicular it points.

questions.stem: Equipotential surfaces are drawn for EQUAL steps of potential (for example every 3 volts). Near a point charge the surfaces are crowded close together; far away they are widely spaced. What does the crowding tell you?

options.A: The field is strong where the surfaces are crowded and weak where they are spread out
options.B: The field is weak where the surfaces are crowded and strong where they are spread out
options.C: The field has the same strength everywhere, since the V-steps are equal
options.D: The crowding shows where more charge is located, not anything about the field

distractor_misconceptions.B: inverts the spacing rule - reads crowded surfaces as a weak field instead of a strong one
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks equal V-steps mean equal field, ignoring that the radii are unequal (r ~ 1/V)
distractor_misconceptions.D: reads the crowding as a charge-distribution map rather than a field-strength map

questions.tested_idea: For equal V-steps r ~ 1/V, so crowded surfaces mark a strong field.
questions.parallel_form_stem: On a contour map of potential drawn at equal voltage intervals, what does the spacing of the contours reveal about the strength of the electric field?

questions.stem: Can two different equipotential surfaces (say V = 8 and V = 6) ever cross or touch?

options.A: No - a single point cannot have two different values of potential at once
options.B: Yes - they cross wherever the field is strongest
options.C: Yes - they touch at the point charge itself
options.D: Yes - they meet far from the charge where V approaches zero

distractor_misconceptions.B: invents a crossing point tied to field strength, which would assign two V values to one point
distractor_misconceptions.C: imagines the surfaces converging at the charge, where in fact V diverges, not converges
distractor_misconceptions.D: imagines distinct surfaces merging far out as V approaches zero, which they never do

questions.tested_idea: Two distinct equipotential surfaces can never intersect - one point holds one V.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain whether the V = 10 and V = 5 equipotential surfaces of a point charge can ever share a common point.

STATE_1.label: One surface, one V - single V=8 shell blooms through P

STATE_2.label: The whole family - nested spheres V=12,8,6,4

STATE_3.label: No work along - tangential slide + cos90 glyph

STATE_4.label: PRIMARY aha - field lines pierce shells at 90, high-V to low-V

STATE_5.label: Crowded means strong - equal V-steps 12,9,6,3 bunch near charge

STATE_6.label: Flip the sign - +Q to -Q, geometry unchanged
STATE_6.formula_overlay: sign flips, geometry stays: concentric spheres

STATE_7.label: Explore - drag P, V constant on a shell, jumps across shells
STATE_7.formula_overlay: V = k Q / r  (constant on a shell)
